observations.recommendation:
1.24 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
a. create an Inventory Committee and require to (i) conduct a physical count
on the property, plant and equipment to determine the existence and
condition of the PPEs; (ii) submit the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) duly reconciled with the
accounting and property records;
b. require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to (i) maintain subsidiary
records for PPE items such as PPE ledger cards and property cards,
respectively, starting with current year acquisitions until all items are
covered; and (ii) reconcile the respective records of the Accounting Office
and Property Officer on PPE;
c. direct all concerned Municipal officials to exert effort in the reproduction
of titles on all lots owned by the Municipality and recognize them in the
book’s lands owned by the Municipality using appraised values; and
d. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a depreciation schedule for
its depreciable assets.

2.8 We recommended and the:
a. Municipal Treasurer agreed to post notice of delinquency for
property taxes at the main entrance of the municipal hall building and
in publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay
wherethese properties are located; seek assistance of the concerned
barangays for the issuance of Notice of Delinquency and conduct an
information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes
promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes; and if
warranted, enforce the provision of Section 254 (a) and (b) of the RA
7160 in the collection of delinquent taxes; and
b. Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile with the office of the
Municipal Treasurer the variance noted.

3.12 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive, Sangguniang Bayan and Local
Development Council agreed to continue complying with Department of Budget and
Management (DBM), Department of Finance (DOF), Department of the Interior
and Local Government (DILG) Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 01, dated
November 4, 2020; continue also to invest in high-impact capital expenditure projects
in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund; and implement effectively and
efficiently the proposed projects within the targeted completion period through close
monitoring and supervision to maximize the developmental benefits and the early
enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.

4.9 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and heads of concerned
implementing offices agreed to:
(a) implement without further delay the remaining projects not
completed/implemented in CY 2024; and
(b) continue to monitor and supervise the effective and efficient
implementation of projects funded from various source agencies
within the timelines in the MOA;
(c) continue to be prompt in the return of excess funds to the source
agencies upon completion or termination of the project.

5.16 However, we recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
a. through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation
of barangay officials to support the solid waste management
program of the government by educating their respective
constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable
materials from compostable wastes;
b. through the MENRO, comply with the requirements of the law on
waste segregation by placing trash bins with proper labels and by
collecting only that garbage that is properly segregated;
c. recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) policies and possible
measures that will ensure full compliance by the barangays with the
provisions of R.A. No. 9003;
d. initiate the implementation of Anti-Littering policy ordinances as
provided by Section 48 thereof;
e. provide support to the concerned barangay for the construction of
secured fence for the MRF for security, health and sanitation
purposes; and
f. implement the plan of issuing Citation Tickets for violators of the
Municipal Environmental Code to strengthen the drive for a more
aggressive solid waste management program.

6.17 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a)
LDRRM Officer to (i) include, under a separate caption, the list of projects and
activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in the LDRRMIFP;
and (ii) continue utilizing the fund for the implementation of projects and activities
for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and
recovery.

7.7 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive, as Chairman of the LSB,
agreed to (a) instruct the Local Accountant, to the DepEd CO, through appropriate
channels, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the
DBM and DILG; (b) instruct the Municipal Budget Officer, as the School Board
Secretary, to monitor and ensure the submission and posting of the SEF Annual
Budget and SEF Utilization Reports, both quarterly and annually, pursuant to Item
6.1 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and (c) instruct the
Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Office to ensure that
accurate SEF Utilization Reports are prepared and posted.

8.9 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) continually
comply with the GAD mechanisms and process to sustain the LGU’s critical
consciousness in supporting gender and development; (b) require the GAD Focal
Person to comply with the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 particularly on
the conduct of gender audit and gender mainstreaming in the Municipality; and (c)
submit the documentary requirements supporting the GAD Accomplishment Report.

9.10 We recommend and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. the immediate settlement or liquidation of outstanding cash
advances in full as of the end of the year except for petty cash
advances which should be liquidated within twenty (20) days
thereafter;
b. monitor the granting and liquidation of cash advances and refrain
from granting additional cash advances unless the previous ones are
liquidated/settled; and
c. reconcile the balances between the GL and Balance per Report on
Unliquidated Cash Advance and prepare Subsidiary Ledger to
support the GL balance.

10.15 We recommended and Local Chief Executive agreed to require the HRMO to
(a) come up with concrete policy guidelines relative to the hiring of JO personnel to
establish a clear expectation and protect the rights of the employees and the
municipality, and (b) prepare contract of service or memorandum of agreement for
all the JOs and have it notarized.

11.5 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed instruct the
concerned officials to submit/furnish the Audit Team copies of Contracts, Purchase
Orders and all supporting documents forming part thereof pursuant to COA
Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001.

12.8 We recommended and the General Services Officer, as far as practicable and
feasible, agreed to procure directly from the PS-DBM all anticipated common-use
supplies, and resort to local suppliers using the prescribed modes of procurement only
upon unavailability of supplies needed as certified by the PS-DBM official.

13.7 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to (i) furnish the monthly Schedule of
Unreleased Checks to the Municipal Accountant; (ii) maintain a
record of the date of release of all checks issued to the payee;
b. Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry to revert back the cash
balance of unreleased checks at-year end, if any; and
c. Proper coordination between Treasury and Accounting Offices to
monitor the actual status of checks issued by the Treasury so that
appropriate adjustments could be made in a timely manner.

14.8 However, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive to:
a. requires the BPLO to instruct the taxpayers to fully and properly
accomplish their duly notarized applications for business permit and
require the submission of audited financial statements in support of
the gross receipts that they declare; and
b. require the Municipal Treasurer’s office to submit list of tax payers
who fulfilled their obligation to the BPLO to identify those who were
delinquent and ensure collection from them.

15.6 We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed, to continue the
withholding and remittance of contributions and repayments of loan amortizations
deducted from the salaries of regular and permanent employees, as well as the prompt
remittance thereof within the prescribed period, in adherence to Section 6 (b) of
Republic Act No. 8291.

16.5 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (a) continue to comply with the
deduction of Pag-ibig contributions of its employees; and (b) promptly remit all
deductions made.

17.5 We recommended and the Accounting and Treasury Office agreed to continue
to comply with the deduction and remittance of PhilHealth contributions of its
employees within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 18 f Republic Act No.7875
and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

18.4 We recommended and the Management agreed to continue to withhold taxes
and check that all taxes withheld are remitted intact and immediately to BIR within
the prescribed period as required by Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007
dated March 23, 2007.

observations.recommendation:
However, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
(a) each Department to prepare their Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for all PPE issued to their office;
(b) GSO to maintain property cards for all PPE owned by the Municipality;
(c) the Municipal Accountant and the GSO (i) reconcile the records of the
Accounting Office and Property Officer on PPE and (ii) submit the RPCPPE
duly reconciled with the accounting and property records to the Inventory
Committee;
(d) the Inventory Committee to (i) conduct physical count of PPE to determine the
existence and condition thereof; and (ii) follow the new COA Circular 2020-
006 re: Guidelines and Procedures in the Conduct of Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE), Recognition of PPE Items Found at
Station, and Disposition for Non-existing/Missing PPE Items, for the One-
Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies; and
(e) concerned Municipal officials to exert effort in the reproduction of titles on all
lots owned by the Municipality and recognize these in the books using the
appraised values.

However, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Health Office to (a) prepare and submit to the Audit Team a detailed
record of received and issued vaccines; and (b) henceforth, ensure the preparation
and maintenance of complete and accurate records and reports.

Likewise, we also recommended that the Municipal Health Officer furnish the
Audit Team with a copy of a thoroughly reviewed master list of vaccinated individuals
for reference and evaluation purposes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) heads of
concerned implementing offices to explain the failure to comply with the local budget
circular; (b) (i) implement without further delay those remaining projects not
completed/implemented in CY 2022; and (ii) continue to monitor and supervise the
effective and efficient implementation of projects funded from various source
agencies within the timelines of the MOA, as far as feasible; (c) Municipal Planning
and Development Officer to update the uploaded data on the website and make the
necessary posting as part of the public disclosure requirements; and (d) Municipal
Accountant and the Treasurer to continue to be prompt in the return of excess funds
to the source agencies upon completion or termination of the project.

However, we also recommended that Management (a) assess its existing
organizational structure so that the ideal number of plantilla personnel will be
established, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive number of job order personnel;
and (b) resort to the hiring of job order personnel based on necessity in order to avoid
waste of much needed funds, as prescribed in Section 76 of RA 7160; and (c) strictly
adhere to the policies and guidelines in the hiring of personnel under job order status
as provided in CSC Memorandum No. 17, s. 2002 promulgating CSC Resolution No.
02-0790 dated June 5, 2002.

However, we also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to
require the (a) MDRRMC to prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) indicating the (i) detailed
PPAs and the corresponding appropriation classified as either Maintenance and
Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) or Capital Outlay (CO); and (ii) under a
separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended
LDRRMF of previous years for ease of monitoring of project implementation, and
(b) MDRRMC ensure that the LDRRMF be strictly utilized for the implementation
of projects and activities for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness,
response and rehabilitation and recovery.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Management (a)
through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay
officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by
educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or
recyclable materials from compostable wastes; (b) through the ESMO, comply with
the requirements of the law on waste segregation by placing trash bins with proper
labels and by collecting only those garbage that are properly segregated; (c) ensure
that all barangays had existing and operational MRF; (d) recommend to the
Sangguniang Bayan (SB) policies and possible measures that will ensure full
compliance by the barangays on the provisions of R.A. No. 9003; (e) initiate the
implementation of its Anti-Littering policy ordinances as provided by Section 48
thereof; (f) review every two years or as the need arises the Municipal Solid Waste
Management Plan for the purpose of ensuring its sustainability, viability,
effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the
field of solid waste management; (g) provide support to the concerned barangay for
the construction of secured fence for the MRF for security, health and sanitation
purposes; and (h) implement the plan of issuing Citation Tickets for violators of the
Municipal Environmental Code to strengthen the drive for a more aggressive solid
waste management program.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) continually
comply with the GAD mechanisms and process to sustain the LGU’s critical
consciousness in supporting gender and development and women’s empowerment;
(b) require the GAD Focal Person to comply with the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM
and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, as amended, and COA Circular No.
2014-001, particularly on the conduct of gender audit and gender mainstreaming in the
Municipality; and (c) submit the documentary requirements supporting the GAD Accomplishment Report.

We also recommended that Management continue to enforce collection
remedies allowed by the pertinent provisions of RA No. 7160, particularly the sale of
delinquent properties through public auction. This will demonstrate the
Municipality’s willpower and capability to enforce the laws on real property taxation
and deter tax evasions to further increase collections and decrease tax delinquencies.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
concerned officials to submit/furnish the Audit Team copies of Contracts, Purchase
Orders and all the supporting documents forming part thereof pursuant to COA
Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001.

We recommended that the General Services Office procure, as far as
practicable and feasible, directly from the PS-DBM all anticipated common-use
supplies, and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of supplies needed
using the prescribed alternative modes of procurement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:
(a) General Services Office (GSO) to: (a) prepare inventory reports of all
insurable properties and (b) submit the inventory reports of all insurable
properties to the GSIS to ensure that all assets and properties are adequately
covered/insured with the GIF of the GSIS to properly protect the government
properties in the event of serious loss such as fire, earthquake, typhoon and/or
other casualties;
(b) Accountable officials of the concerned offices with insurable properties to
coordinate with the General Services Office (GSO) regarding insurance of
properties under their jurisdiction; and
(c) Inclusion in its annual appropriation the amount necessary to cover the
premiums for the insurance of its properties during each fiscal period and
remit the same immediately to the GSIS.

We commended the efforts of Management and recommended that it
continue to unfailingly remit the amounts due to the GSIS within the prescribed
period in adherence to Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291 and its Implementing
Rules and Regulations. However, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant,
Human Resource Management Officer and the Municipal Treasurer review and
reconcile the amount as of the year end.

We commended Management and recommended that it sustain efforts in the
unfailing remittance of the amounts due to the Home Development Mutual Fund or
Pag-IBIG Fund within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 3(a) of
Republic Act No. 9769 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We commended Management and recommended that its Accounting and
Treasury Office continue to comply with the deduction and remittance of PhilHealth
contributions of its employees within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 18(b)
of Republic Act No.7875 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommended that Management (a) continue being compliant with the
withholding of the appropriate taxes and the remittance thereof within the prescribed
period; and (b) exert effort to reconcile the difference between the record of the
Municipality and the records of the BIR.

We recommended that the
a. Local Chief Executive explain the failure to comply with LBC 131;
b. Municipal Accountant maintain a Special Account in the General Fund for the BGCM; and
c. Municipal Planning and Development Officer update the uploaded data on the website and make the necessary posting as part of the public disclosure requirements.

We commended the efforts of Management and recommended that it continue to unfailingly remit the amounts due the GSIS within the prescribed period in adherence to Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We commended Management and recommended that it sustain efforts in the unfailing remittance of the amounts due the Home Development Mutual Fund or Pag-IBIG Fund within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 3(a) of Republic Act No. 9769 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We commended Management and recommended that its Accounting and Treasury Office continue to comply with the deduction and remittance of Philhealth contributions of its employees within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 18(b) of Republic Act No.7875 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.
We recommended that the Municipality continue to being compliant in the withholding of the appropriate taxes and the remittance thereof within the prescribed time.

We recommended that management (a) prepare and issue policy guidelines to address the necessity and propriety of hiring of job orders pursuant to Section 2, Rule XI of the Revised Omnibus on Appointments and Other Personnel Actions, Rules Implementing Book V of EO No. 292, in order to limit the expenditures to programs that are highly essential to merit the recruitment of the same; (b) avoid the hiring of job orders whose functions overlap/duplicate with those of the regular employees of the Municipality; (c) review the daily rates granted to job orders which should be commensurate to the nature of work assigned them; and (d) ensure that the Statement of Duties and Responsibilities are stipulated in the contract of service in order to match their skills against the work to be performed.
We recommended that the SB members prioritize the passing and approval of the Revised Revenue Code to increase the Municipality’s revenue for the benefit of its constituents.

observations.recommendation:
1.9    We recommended that the Local Chief Executive -
reconstitute an Inventory Committee to include each Department Head to facilitate inventory taking by Office of all the property, plant and equipment of the Municipality;
require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain Property Records  starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’ acquisitions and aim for a yearly increase of coverage until all PPE items are covered; (ii) reconcile inventory results with property records and those maintained at the Accounting Office and isolate items not reconciled for easy reference in succeeding inventory taking; (iii) aim to reduce the number and amount of unreconciled PPE account balances
direct the Acting Municipal Treasure to prepare Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE) for all PPE items, starting with current and recent years’ acquisitions until all items are covered and thereafter, renew the AREs at least every three years or whenever there is change of custodianship; and
instruct the Inventory Committee to check the condition of the PPE and prepare a report thereon in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and Waste Materials Reports (WMR), the damaged, obsolete and unserviceable PPE identified in the course of inventory-taking and request for its immediate disposal.

2.5   	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require a written explanation from the Acting Municipal Treasurer for the noted delays in the deposit and the retention of collections  within unreasonable period; and (b)  direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections regularly and intact within the  prescribed period to the authorized government depository banks as required in Section 69 of P.D. 1445 and other existing regulations to avoid possible theft/loss or misuse.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, thru the Municipal Accountant and Acting Treasurer  (a)  maintain the Petty Cash Fund  under the imprest system and follow the procedures for petty cash disbursements in accordance with the Manual on the New Government Accounting System to avoid unauthorized disbursements from the fund; (b) limit to petty expenses and those which cannot be paid thru checks the amounts charged to Petty Cash Fund; (c) stop the payment of regular expenses out of the PCF; and (d)  prepare separate disbursement vouchers for recurring expenses and pay them thru checks, including the cash advances to be drawn by an authorized disbursing officer, for the preparation of payroll for duly approved/screened beneficiaries of financial assistance.

4.7   	We recommended that the (a)  Municipal Treasurer prepare the certified list of taxpayers based on the Assessment Roll from the Municipal Assessor’s Office starting with active taxpayers and expanding the roll by aiming to increase it annually until all taxpayers are covered;  and (b) Municipal Accountant  set up the RPT/SET Receivable based on the Certified List of Taxpayers and provide and maintain subsidiary ledgers of individual taxpayer, starting with those active taxpayers, to support the general ledger balances and facilitate monitoring and reconciliation of tax collections and delinquencies.

6.5     	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the members of the Bids and Awards Committee, including the BAC Secretariat, to (a) render an explanation or justification on the resort to shopping instead of thru competitive bidding in the procurement of the subject contracts; (b) henceforth, adhere to Sections 48.2 and 54.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184  (2016 edition) on the general policy on competitive bidding and prohibition of splitting of contracts; and (c) resort only to alternative modes of procurement if the conditions are met as provided under Section 48, 52, and 53.9 of the IRR of RA No. 9184.

7.12 	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution establishing specific guidelines on the rates or amounts of financial assistance being extended by the Municipality and the criteria for the entitlement for each type thereof to rationalize disbursements under the Donations account; (b)  require the (i) Municipal Treasurer to strictly follow the regulation on payment through check directly to the recipients/beneficiaries; (ii) Municipal Social Welfare Officer to evaluate and screen properly the recipients by giving priority to the less privileged constituents; and (iii) Municipal Accountant henceforth  ensure that all payments are supported with complete/proper documentation.

8.10   	We recommended that the  Local Chief Executive  (a) limit the procurement of goods and services on a reimbursement basis, unless the circumstances warrant its application; (b) follow strictly the rules and procedures of   procurement  provided in the Revised IRR of RA 9184 2016 edition; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that payments are made by check directly to the creditors to whom payments are due and withhold the corresponding tax. Regular and recurring expenses should not be paid through reimbursement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) coordinate efforts with the barangay officials on the implementation of the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003;  (b) instruct the Solid Waste Management Officer to closely monitor the proper maintenance of the MRFs; (c) comply with the submission of the additional requirements specified in the NSWC evaluation checklist for the approval of its Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan; (c) strictly enforce the Sangguniang Bayan ordinance on the “no segregation, no collection” policy.; and (d) intensify information drive and awareness campaign on the regulations on solid waste management in coordination with the DepEd officials in the Municipality and barangay officials.

12.7  We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant (a) strictly follow the Revised Chart of Accounts for the LGUs by using the appropriate accounts based on their definitions in the preparation of the Obligation Request (ObR) and the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to avoid erroneous/misleading entries and to provide accurate and reliable account balances in the financial statements; and (b) draw appropriate journal entries to correct the misclassification especially those affecting real accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to (a) continue to invest in high-impact development projects in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund so that the best possible desired level of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes may be achieved/attained and refrain from proposing projects that do not partake the nature of investments and capital expenditures; and (b)  ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period through close supervision and monitoring to maximize the developmental benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive
prioritize the hiring of a full-time MDRRM Officer while awaiting the creation of the positon;
direct the Accountant to  prepare a certified monthly Report on the Utilization of DRRMF and submit to  COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and
instruct the MDRRM officer to refrain from using DRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not directly related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations

15.5  	We recommended that the LCE and project implements (a) be compliant with Good Financial Housekeeping so that the Municipality will continue to be a recipient of identified BuB projects; and (b) exercise close supervision and monitoring to achieve effective and efficient implementation of the projects within their targeted date of completion to maximize the benefits and early enjoyment thereof of the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to submit/furnish the Audit Team copies of Contracts, Purchase Orders and all supporting documents forming part thereof pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001.
18.4  	We recommended that the Municipality continue to being compliant in the withholding of taxes from compensation and payments to suppliers and the remittance thereof within the prescribed time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to submit without delay the liquidation documents of the cash advances totaling P6,874,200.00 and follow the accounting procedure in recording cash advances provided in the New Chart of Accounts; and

We also recommended that the concerned SB members settle the unliquidated cash advance of P46,000.00.
